Construct a precision matrix for a grid

Source: R/sim.R
dot-get_precision_matrix.Rd

Constructs a spatial precision matrix for an admove_grid, either from a Matérn covariance structure or from a simple neighbour-based structure.

Usage

.get_precision_matrix(grid, h, nu, rho, delta, matern = TRUE)

Arguments

grid

A grid object of class "admove_grid", as returned by create_grid().

h

Distance argument used internally in the Matérn covariance structure.

nu

Smoothness parameter of the Matérn covariance structure.

rho

Spatial range parameter of the Matérn covariance structure.

delta

Small positive value added to the diagonal to ensure numerical stability and positive definiteness.

matern

Logical; if TRUE, a Matérn-based covariance structure is used. Otherwise, a neighbour-based graph Laplacian is constructed.

Value

A precision matrix for the active cells of the grid.

Details

When matern = TRUE, pairwise distances between grid-cell centres are used to build a Matérn covariance matrix, which is then inverted to obtain a precision matrix. When matern = FALSE, a sparse neighbour-based precision matrix is constructed from the grid adjacency structure.

In both cases, delta * I is added to the diagonal.

On this page

Developed by Tobias Mildenberger, Mark Maunder, Anders Nielsen.
